title: Sawles Warde
summary: The repository XML is a local copy or version of University of Michigan CME item Sawleswd, which carries OTA identifier U-1323-A. The current Oxford Text Archive item preserves John Cooper's 8 August 1989 deposit statement that the text was supplied ‘as seen on CTILIB.’ Although the U-M header names R. M. Wilson's 1938 edition as its source, the electronic page milestones 165–186 and direct text comparison instead strongly support Simonne d'Ardenne's 1977 Bodley-based Katherine Group edition as the probable print ancestor; the exact CTILIB conversion remains undocumented. The abstract medieval work is an early-thirteenth-century alliterative prose homily surviving in Bodley 34, Royal 17 A XXVII, and Cotton Titus D XVIII. The electronic stream appends Royal's scribal colophon and nine-line rhymed prayer after the homily; those are witness-specific paratext and do not make the abstract work mixed-form. Complete public Wilson, Huber–Robertson, and Kano sources and exact OTA, Helsinki, and official Bodleian catalog files are cached. No public complete manuscript-image set was found: Bodley has a complete 1960 photographic facsimile behind controlled/onsite access, Royal's catalog reports historical full coverage but exposes no live images, and Cotton's legacy complete-image route is explicitly unavailable.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_from_OTA/Sawleswd.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1993 U-M electronic item, OTA U-1323-A, anonymous work, inherited Wilson 1938 source claim, bare 1240 legacy date, stated omissions, and a text consisting of the homily followed by Royal's Johan colophon and nine-line prayer.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, Sawleswd item.
evidence.summary: Confirms the persistent Sawleswd item, public web access, title, anonymous attribution, U-M/HTI responsibility, and repeated Wilson source description.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, rights statement for CME item Sawleswd.
evidence.summary: Labels the item Materials in this collection are in the public domain and No Copyright–United States, while retaining provider terms around the interface and collection.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, Sawles Warde, item 1323, exact TEI header and deposited text files.
evidence.locator: Deposit email dated 8 August 1989; complete text following the email
evidence.summary: Confirms John Cooper's 8 August 1989 deposit ‘as seen on CTILIB,’ the exact 25,910-byte text stream, current persistent identifier, and current CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 distribution. The separately cached TEI header is 4,469 bytes with SHA-256 f2f74d05a30ceea839591c2bafd24b3a82bf7e8d07b00e06a567fce4371ec914.

evidence.citation: Direct normalized comparison of the General Corpus XML, exact OTA 1323 deposit, and Helsinki Corpus sward sample, performed 15 July 2026.
evidence.locator: Complete OTA stream; repository BODY; Helsinki pp. 165.20–183.24
evidence.summary: After decoding legacy character conventions and comparing alphabetic streams, OTA and the repository XML agree at a SequenceMatcher ratio of 0.9997972630511911, with only two short replacement regions (OTA acouemmge versus XML acouerunge; OTA snch versus XML swuch). Word-sequence comparison of the d'Ardenne-based Helsinki sample against the corresponding electronic passage is approximately 0.9315, with differences dominated by word division and orthography; both preserve the 165–183 page sequence.
notes: The comparison strongly supports a shared stream and d'Ardenne pagination but cannot by itself identify who created the CTILIB text or document every conversion step.

evidence.citation: CiNii Books, The Katherine Group: Edited from MS. Bodley 34, NCID BA13068656.
evidence.summary: Confirms d'Ardenne's responsibility, 1977 Paris publication by Société d'Édition Les Belles Lettres, series fascicle 215, xii + 185 pages, Middle English language, Sawles warde among the contents, and seven listed library holdings.

evidence.citation: Helsinki Corpus of English Texts, Sawles Warde sample sward and exact TEI source.
evidence.locator: teiHeader source description and profile; sample pages 165.20–183.24
evidence.summary: Explicitly identifies S. R. T. O. d'Ardenne, The Katherine Group (1977), pp. 165.20–183.24, as source for a 3,831-word sample and provides a text stream aligned with OTA/U-M.

evidence.citation: Helsinki Corpus of English Texts, Copyright and permitted use.
evidence.summary: States that the corpus is protected, permits duplication for research and educational purposes, and requires permission for other uses.

evidence.citation: R. M. Wilson, ed., Sawles Warde: An Early Middle English Homily (Leeds, 1938), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1–162; introduction pp. i–xlvi; text and apparatus printed pp. 1–115
evidence.summary: Provides the complete named source edition, exact three-witness presentation, manuscript descriptions, approximate 1210–15 or earlier work dating, West Midlands and possible Herefordshire localization, argument for rhythmic/alliterative prose, Royal-only colophon and prayer, reconstructed Z–X–B/R and Z–T stemma, and the historically superseded Hugh/De anima source attribution.
notes: The provider PDF has recoverable structural warnings; pdfinfo and full page/text extraction succeed.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for ltm-3-4-wilson-sawles-warde-dickins-witand-will.
evidence.summary: Confirms the item identifier, 1938 date, 189-page PDF filename and size, complete OCR and scan-data derivatives, ARK, and uploader-supplied CC BY-ND 4.0 license URL.

evidence.citation: Emily Rebekah Huber and Elizabeth Robertson, eds. and trans., The Katherine Group (Medieval English Texts Series, 2016), complete PDF.
evidence.summary: A complete modern Bodley-based edition, translation, and notes supporting the work's allegorical homiletic content, prose form, adaptation of De custodia interioris hominis, audience context, Bodley/Royal transition, witness changes, Royal-only colophon and nine-line prayer, and other editorial interventions.
notes: The provider PDF has recoverable structural warnings; pdfinfo and full page/text extraction succeed.

evidence.citation: Medieval English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation.
evidence.summary: Permits individual use, requires permission for reprinting, and separately licenses downloadable XML data under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0; it does not state that every PDF or all editorial matter carries that XML license.

evidence.citation: Akihiko Kano, Sawles Warde in Three Parallel Texts (2006), complete Komazawa University repository PDF.
evidence.locator: Complete article, pp. 269–350; manuscript reinspection and three parallel texts
evidence.summary: Provides a complete three-witness comparison and reports renewed inspection of Royal and Cotton in 2005, correcting readings in Wilson and earlier editions.
notes: The provider PDF has recoverable hint-table warnings; pdfinfo reports 82 pages and full text extraction succeeds.

evidence.citation: Andoni Cossio and Nelson Goering, ‘J. R. R. Tolkien's Soul's Ward: A Critical Edition of His Unpublished Translation of the Early Middle English Homily Sawles Warde,’ Review of English Studies, hgag038 (8 June 2026).
evidence.locator: Complete published article, pp. 1–17; introduction; ‘Soul's Ward and other related manuscripts’; editorial practices
evidence.summary: The University of Glasgow Enlighten record supplies the exact 17-page, 638,341-byte published version under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. The article identifies an early-thirteenth-century Middle English prose homily, Tolkien's approximate 1210 dating, West Midlands AB language, free adaptation of De custodia interioris hominis for anchorites and cloistered nuns, exact witness loci B 72r–80v, R 1r–10v, T 105v–112v, and d'Ardenne's Bodley-centered editorial method with Royal/Cotton supplies. The exact repository landing HTML is cached separately at source-cache/Sawleswd/cossio-goering-2026-enlighten-record.html with SHA-256 fb6a4fc6435f66a4a27b0ad0a3880aedd5231364acfb8d0e5a061d6017e975f8.
notes: The cached PDF is the unchanged Glasgow-hosted published version; pdfinfo reports 17 pages and qpdf validation is clean.
Because the license forbids derivatives, neither the PDF nor the cached landing HTML was transformed.

evidence.citation: Oxford University Press article and PDF endpoints for Cossio and Goering 2026, automated access check.
evidence.summary: Both direct OUP endpoints returned HTTP 403 from the research environment. No challenge was bypassed and no OUP-hosted object was cached; the official University of Glasgow Enlighten record and exact published PDF provide the documented fallback.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Catalogue of Western Medieval Manuscripts, MS Bodley 34, official TEI source.
evidence.locator: Sawles Warde item ff. 72r–80v; physical description; origin; bibliography
evidence.summary: Confirms Sawles Warde at ff. 72r–80v, two leaves missing after fol. 80, Middle English AB language, first-half-thirteenth-century South-West Midlands origin, low-certainty possible Hereford association, 81-leaf codex structure, and the complete 1960 photographic facsimile.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Sawles Warde [Middle English], work record 14494.
evidence.summary: Confirms the preferred work title, Middle English language, theology subject, and Bodley 34 as the currently cataloged witness, while warning that online coverage may not be comprehensive.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.
evidence.summary: Confirms that users may request reference-quality PDF scans, subject to legal, conservation, quantity, and other exceptions and Imaging Services terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Using special collections.
evidence.summary: Explains Group A Reader-card access and Weston Library consultation for special collections.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, Facsimile of MS. Bodley 34, 1960, catalog record 40715.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete 1960 EETS OS 247 photographic facsimile, its Katherine Group contents and N. R. Ker introduction, and an onsite library copy.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive records for two controlled copies of Facsimile of MS Bodley 34.
evidence.summary: Item metadata marks both copies access restricted; unencrypted PDFs are private and only encrypted controlled-loan derivatives are exposed publicly.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 A XXVII and component ff. 1–70.
evidence.locator: Whole-codex Scope & Content, Access, Information About Copies; component Contents, Date Range, User Conditions, and colophon
evidence.summary: Confirms a 97-leaf parchment codex, the first-half-thirteenth-century West Midlands Katherine Group part, Sawles Warde at ff. 1r–10v, the Johan colophon and nine-line prayer on f. 10v, letter-of-introduction requirement, physical consultation route, and a historical statement of full digital coverage despite a blank current Digitised Content field.

evidence.citation: Direct checks of the historical British Library Royal MS 17 A XXVII FullDisplay and Viewer routes.
evidence.summary: The historical routes do not expose a current manuscript image inventory or viewer; the interim catalog's Digitised Content field is blank as of the review date.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Titus D XVIII and component ff. 14–147.
evidence.locator: Whole-codex Contents, Digitised Content, Access, and Physical Characteristics; component Contents, Date Range, and User Conditions
evidence.summary: Confirms a composite parchment codex, first-half-thirteenth-century Middle English component ff. 14–147, Sawles Warde at ff. 105v–112v, physical dimensions and layout, letter-of-introduction requirement, physical consultation route, and a legacy full-display link explicitly labeled ‘digital images currently unavailable.’

evidence.citation: Direct check of the British Library Cotton MS Titus D XVIII legacy FullDisplay route.
evidence.summary: The legacy route does not expose a current image inventory, and the current whole-codex catalog explicitly marks the digital images unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Provides the image-order route, required shelfmark/title/folio information, reference-versus-reproduction options, surrogate and colour caveats, payment requirement, and separate licensing warning.

open_questions.question: What exact CTILIB object and print source produced the 1989 OTA deposit, and when were d'Ardenne's page milestones added?
next_steps: Locate surviving CTILIB documentation or backups and identify the exact object seen by John Cooper on 8 August 1989.
Compare a physical copy of d'Ardenne 1977, pp. 165–186, character by character with the OTA deposit and U-M page boundaries.
Ask OTA and U-M whether migration or conversion logs survive for item 1323 / U-1323-A.
notes: The current evidence strongly favors d'Ardenne 1977 over Wilson 1938 as the print ancestor but does not document the full transfer chain.

open_questions.question: Can a complete public, auditable manuscript-image inventory be recovered for Bodley 34, Royal 17 A XXVII, or Cotton Titus D XVIII?
next_steps: Ask the Bodleian whether any complete digital Bodley 34 surrogate exists beyond the controlled 1960 printed facsimile and whether complete-codex mediated copying is feasible.
Ask the British Library whether Royal's historically reported full coverage and Cotton's legacy coverage can be restored or supplied, and request canvas/page inventories before claiming completeness.
If ordering images, request complete codices rather than cropped Sawles Warde folios unless the task is explicitly work-only.

open_questions.question: Which recension and physical exemplar of De custodia interioris hominis underlies Sawles Warde?
next_steps: Consult Becker's source studies and current research on the pseudo-Anselmian De moribus tradition.
Do not substitute the obsolete Hugh of Saint-Victor / De anima attribution or claim an extant physical Latin exemplar without textual evidence.

open_questions.question: How should the U-M No Copyright–US statement, OTA CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license, inherited modern edition, and unresolved CTILIB encoding be reconciled for downstream reuse?
next_steps: Request item-specific provenance and rights clarification from U-M and OTA.
Separate reuse of public-domain medieval readings from reuse of modern editorial selections, encoding, metadata, and photography.
Do not infer that one provider's rights statement grants rights in every upstream layer.

rights.component: The underlying anonymous medieval homily and medieval witness readings, including Royal's medieval colophon and prayer, but excluding modern editing, encoding, catalog prose, and photography
rights.basis: The work and all three surviving manuscript witnesses date from the early or first half of the thirteenth century, centuries before modern copyright terms.
notes: This does not authorize reuse of a modern edition, digital encoding, catalog description, manuscript photograph, or provider interface.

rights.component: Repository XML structure and inherited electronic text beyond the public-domain medieval readings
rights.basis: The artifact inherits modern electronic encoding from an unresolved CTILIB/OTA/U-M chain and conflicting source-edition metadata; no repository-local grant resolves every upstream layer.

rights.component: U-M digital item as presented by the provider
rights.basis: U-M labels the item ‘No Copyright – United States’ and ‘Materials in this collection are in the public domain.’
notes: This records the provider's item-level assertion; it does not prove a complete upstream rights chain for the encoding or source edition.

rights.component: OTA item 1323 metadata and distributed electronic files
rights.basis: The current OTA item page distributes the record under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0.

rights.component: d'Ardenne's 1977 editorial matter, transcription choices, introduction, and apparatus
rights.basis: A 1977 scholarly edition by a modern editor is not presumed public domain, and no open license was found in the consulted catalog evidence.

rights.component: Wilson's modern editorial matter, introduction, reconstructed text, notes, and glossary
rights.basis: The 1938 publication date alone does not establish public-domain status in every jurisdiction, and renewal/publication facts were not fully researched.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan object and derivatives, distinct from the underlying medieval text and Wilson's edition
rights.basis: Internet Archive item metadata supplies a CC BY-ND 4.0 license URL, but the uploader's authority to license all underlying editorial content was not independently established.

rights.component: Helsinki Corpus Sawles Warde sample and annotation
rights.basis: The corpus copyright page states that the corpus is protected and permits duplication for research and educational purposes while requiring permission for other use.

rights.component: Huber and Robertson's modern edition, translation, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: The METS copyright page permits individual use and requires permission for reprinting; its separate CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 statement applies to downloadable XML data and is not generalized here to the PDF or all editorial matter.

rights.component: Cossio and Goering's complete published article and critical edition of Tolkien's translation
rights.basis: The University of Glasgow Enlighten record identifies the authors as copyright holders and the deposited published version as CC BY-NC-ND 4.0; the same license is stated in the exact published PDF.
notes: The exact published PDF and exact Enlighten landing HTML are cached unchanged; no transformation was made under the NoDerivatives restriction.

rights.component: Kano's 2006 article, editorial transcription, and parallel-text arrangement
rights.basis: The public repository permits access to a modern scholarly article; no open reuse license was found on the cached object or record.

rights.component: The 1960 photographic facsimile, Ker's introduction, layout, and modern photography, excluding the underlying medieval manuscript text
rights.basis: The facsimile is a 1960 publication distributed through controlled access; no open license was found.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photographs and legacy digital surrogate
rights.basis: No current image inventory, image-level license, or reusable files were exposed; catalog metadata licensing cannot be transferred to unseen photographs.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photographs and legacy digital surrogate
rights.basis: The catalog states that images are unavailable and exposes no image-level license or reusable files; catalog metadata licensing cannot be transferred to unseen photographs.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bodley 34 base text with missing or defective readings and two missing terminal leaves
editorial_practices.representation: Bodley-centered transcription; missing letters and words supplied from Royal, Cotton, or both; other variant readings relegated to notes
editorial_practices.description: d'Ardenne transcribes Bodley while using Royal and Cotton selectively to repair loss and record variants.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three divergent manuscript witnesses, with Bodley's terminal loss and witness-specific endings
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel diplomatic texts and apparatus, plus a reconstructed stemma
editorial_practices.description: Wilson prints the Bodley, Royal, and Cotton texts in parallel and distinguishes Royal's colophon and prayer from the shared homily.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A source edition containing introduction, notes, glossary, and multiple witness/editorial layers
editorial_practices.representation: The electronic item retains the homily plus Royal colophon and prayer while omitting front matter, back matter, and notes
editorial_practices.description: The U-M/OTA stream is a selected textual representation, not a complete digital facsimile of Wilson or d'Ardenne.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete d'Ardenne Sawles Warde text at pp. 165–186
editorial_practices.description: The Helsinki object deliberately samples rather than reproduces the complete work or edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bodley base text with terminal loss and readings supplied from Royal and Cotton
editorial_practices.representation: Modern edited Middle English text, facing translation, witness changes, and explanatory notes
editorial_practices.description: Huber and Robertson present a readable modern Bodley-based edition while documenting changes of witness and the Royal-only ending.

This is an inherited header claim. It conflicts with the electronic 165–186 page milestones and d'Ardenne/Helsinki alignment.

assertion.notes: Wilson may have been consulted or accidentally substituted in later metadata; direct encoding from his pagination is not supported by the current stream.

scope.notes: Bodley is the base manuscript; its terminal loss requires supplementation from other witnesses.

subject.description: PDF pages 1–162 of the 189-page scan object
The anonymous medieval Sawles Warde homily, excluding Royal's witness-specific colophon and prayer

object.description: The conceptual Latin work De custodia interioris hominis; no specific recension or physical exemplar identified

scope.notes: Sawles Warde is a free translation and adaptation, not a direct diplomatic transcription. This intellectual relation does not assert a surviving Latin manuscript exemplar.

assertion.notes: Current scholarship identifies De custodia interioris hominis, associated with but probably not authored by Anselm. Wilson's older Hugh of Saint-Victor / De anima attribution is obsolete; the exact Latin recension remains unresolved.

scope.notes: This supporting descriptive relation is not an upstream textual-source relation.

review_notes: Keep the medieval work, three manuscript witnesses, scholarly editions, electronic encodings, repository artifact, and modern facsimiles as separate entities.
Treat d'Ardenne 1977 as the probable electronic print source and Wilson 1938 as an inherited conflicting claim; do not flatten the two into certainty.
Treat Royal's Johan colophon and nine-line prayer as witness-specific paratext retained by the electronic stream, not as proof that the anonymous homily is a mixed prose-and-verse work.
A complete printed edition that discusses or transcribes all three witnesses is not a complete manuscript facsimile. Manuscript-wide image access was checked separately for Bodley, Royal, and Cotton.
Underlying medieval text, modern editing, digital encoding, catalog prose, modern photography, and provider contract terms are distinct rights layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for Sawleswd
entities.description: The checked-in XML source used by this repository: the complete homily as represented by the electronic stream, followed by the Royal colophon and nine-line prayer, with page milestones 165–186.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item Sawleswd
entities.description: U-M Humanities Text Initiative representation linked to OTA U-1323-A. Its header names Wilson 1938, but its pagination and text stream conflict with that attribution and align with the d'Ardenne/OTA stream.
notes: The current U-M item labels the materials No Copyright–United States. That provider statement does not resolve rights in upstream editing or encoding.
The header's ‘1240’ is not a precise composition date.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive item 1323
entities.description: The current OTA dissemination record and exact deposited plain-text stream. The preserved deposit email says John Cooper supplied it on 8 August 1989 as a text seen on CTILIB.
notes: OTA's cataloged publication interval 1120–1141 is incompatible with the work and witnesses and is retained only as a legacy metadata error, not as a work date.

entities.label: Unidentified CTILIB Sawles Warde display
entities.description: The electronic object or display from which John Cooper said the 1989 OTA deposit was taken. No surviving CTILIB file, documentation, or stable identifier was located.

entities.label: d'Ardenne, The Katherine Group, 1977
alternate_labels: The Katherine Group: Edited from MS. Bodley 34
entities.description: Bodley-based diplomatic edition of the five Katherine Group texts. For Sawles Warde it prints pages 165–186, supplies missing letters and words from Royal or Cotton, and places other collated readings in notes.

bibliographic.citation: S. T. R. O. d'Ardenne, ed., The Katherine Group: Edited from MS. Bodley 34 (Paris: Société d'Édition Les Belles Lettres, 1977), Bibliothèque de la Faculté de philosophie et lettres de l'Université de Liège 215, xii + 185 pages.
bibliographic.title: The Katherine Group: Edited from MS. Bodley 34
bibliographic.series: Bibliothèque de la Faculté de philosophie et lettres de l'Université de Liège 215

entities.label: Wilson, Sawles Warde, 1938
alternate_labels: Sawles Warde: An Early Middle English Homily
entities.description: R. M. Wilson's three-witness diplomatic edition with introduction, notes, glossary, parallel witness texts, the then-assumed Latin source, and a reconstructed stemma. The inherited U-M header names it as source, but the electronic stream does not carry Wilson's pagination.

bibliographic.citation: R. M. Wilson, ed., Sawles Warde: An Early Middle English Homily, Edited from the Bodley, Royal and Cotton MSS. (Leeds: School of English Language, University of Leeds, 1938), Leeds School of English Language Texts and Monographs 3–4, xlvi + 115 pages.
bibliographic.title: Sawles Warde: An Early Middle English Homily, Edited from the Bodley, Royal and Cotton MSS.
bibliographic.series: Leeds School of English Language Texts and Monographs 3–4
bibliographic.publisher: School of English Language, University of Leeds

notes: Wilson's attribution of the Latin source to Hugh of Saint-Victor's De anima is historically important but superseded by the identification of the pseudo-Anselmian De custodia interioris hominis.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Wilson 1938
entities.description: Complete 189-page scan object containing Wilson's full edition followed by Bruce Dickins's Wit and Will; Wilson occupies PDF pages 1–162.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR and scan metadata

entities.label: Helsinki Corpus Sawles Warde sample
entities.description: A 3,831-word sample explicitly sourced from d'Ardenne 1977, pp. 165.20–183.24. It shares the d'Ardenne pagination and aligns closely with the OTA/U-M text stream.

entities.label: Huber and Robertson, The Katherine Group, 2016
entities.description: Complete modern edition, translation, and commentary of the Katherine Group. Sawles Warde is printed at pp. 249–266 with notes at pp. 267–279, based on Bodley and supplied where necessary from Royal and Cotton.

bibliographic.citation: Emily Rebekah Huber and Elizabeth Robertson, eds. and trans., The Katherine Group (Medieval English Texts Series, 2016), Sawles Warde pp. 249–279.
bibliographic.title: The Katherine Group
bibliographic.pages: 358 PDF pages; Sawles Warde printed pp. 249–279

entities.label: Cossio and Goering, Tolkien's Soul's Ward, 2026
entities.description: A scholarly article and critical edition of J. R. R. Tolkien's previously unpublished translation of Sawles Warde, with current discussion of the medieval homily's date, language, Latin source, audience, witnesses, and modern editorial history.

bibliographic.citation: Andoni Cossio and Nelson Goering, ‘J. R. R. Tolkien's Soul's Ward: A Critical Edition of His Unpublished Translation of the Early Middle English Homily Sawles Warde,’ The Review of English Studies, hgag038 (8 June 2026), 1–17, https://doi.org/10.1093/res/hgag038.
bibliographic.title: J. R. R. Tolkien's Soul's Ward: A Critical Edition of His Unpublished Translation of the Early Middle English Homily Sawles Warde

notes: This is a supporting current scholarly source, not an upstream source of the OTA/U-M/General Corpus electronic stream.

entities.label: Kano, Sawles Warde in Three Parallel Texts, 2006
entities.description: A complete scholarly article and three-witness parallel text based on renewed 2005 inspection of the Royal and Cotton manuscripts and comparison with Bodley and prior editions.

bibliographic.citation: Akihiko Kano, ‘Sawles Warde in Three Parallel Texts,’ Komazawa University, 2006, pp. 269–350.
bibliographic.title: Sawles Warde in Three Parallel Texts

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 34
entities.description: The principal Bodley witness of the Katherine Group. Sawles Warde occupies ff. 72r–80v and is imperfect because two leaves are missing after fol. 80.

holding.notes: Sawles Warde ff. 72r–80v; consult in the Weston Library.

notes: Two leaves are missing after fol. 80, so this witness breaks off before the homily's end.
The official catalog describes South-West Midlands AB language and only tentatively associates the manuscript with Hereford.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 A XXVII
entities.description: Composite parchment codex whose first-half-thirteenth-century West Midlands part contains Sawles Warde at ff. 1r–10v. Fol. 10v preserves Johan's scribal colophon and a nine-line rhymed prayer after the homily.

date_statements.label: Relevant manuscript part
date_statements.value: First half of the thirteenth century

holding.notes: Sawles Warde ff. 1r–10v; physical consultation is in London and requires a letter of introduction.

notes: The codex also contains later fifteenth-century prayers and hymns at ff. 71–97.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus D XVIII
entities.description: Composite parchment codex whose first-half-thirteenth-century Middle English component at ff. 14–147 contains Sawles Warde at ff. 105v–112v.

date_statements.label: Relevant manuscript part
date_statements.value: First half of the thirteenth century

holding.notes: Sawles Warde ff. 105v–112v; physical consultation is in London and requires a letter of introduction.

physical_description.extent: 148 leaves in three parts; relevant component ff. 14–147; Sawles Warde ff. 105v–112v
physical_description.format: Relevant component written in two columns of 24–30 lines
notes: The whole codex contains material from the thirteenth through sixteenth centuries; the Sawles Warde component is specifically dated to the first half of the thirteenth century.

entities.label: Reconstructed lost exemplar X
entities.description: Wilson's hypothetical lost intermediary from which he derived the Bodley and Royal branches. It is a reconstructed stemmatic node, not an observed physical manuscript.

entities.label: Reconstructed lost archetype Z
entities.description: Wilson's hypothetical lost archetype above Cotton and the reconstructed X branch. It is a critical reconstruction rather than an extant physical object.

entities.label: Conceptual source work: De custodia interioris hominis
entities.description: The pseudo-Anselmian Latin dialogue or sermon freely translated and expanded in Sawles Warde. This entity represents the conceptual source work only: the exact recension and physical exemplar used by the Middle English adapter have not been identified. Wilson's 1938 attribution to Hugh of Saint-Victor's De anima is retained as a superseded historical claim, not as current source identification.
notes: This catalog description is not a manuscript witness, scholarly edition, or claim about a specific Latin carrier.

entities.label: Facsimile of MS Bodley 34, EETS OS 247 (1960)
entities.description: Printed photographic facsimile of the complete manuscript with an introduction by N. R. Ker. It is the only complete manuscript-wide Bodley image source found, but current digital access is controlled rather than public download.

bibliographic.citation: Facsimile of MS. Bodley 34: St. Katherine, St. Margaret, St. Juliana, Hali Meiðhad, Sawles Warde, with an introduction by N. R. Ker (London: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society, 1960), EETS Original Series 247.
bibliographic.title: Facsimile of MS. Bodley 34
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 247
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: British Library legacy full digital surrogate of Royal MS 17 A XXVII
entities.description: The current catalog preserves the historical statement ‘Full digital coverage available for this manuscript,’ but its Digitised Content field is blank and the legacy FullDisplay/Viewer routes expose no image inventory.
entities.reproduction_format: Digital manuscript page images; exact current inventory unavailable
notes: Historical completeness is reported by the catalog; current completeness and access cannot be independently audited without recovering the image inventory.

entities.label: British Library legacy full digital surrogate of Cotton MS Titus D XVIII
entities.description: The whole-codex catalog links a legacy FullDisplay page but explicitly labels the digital images currently unavailable.
entities.reproduction_format: Digital manuscript page images; currently inaccessible
notes: No image inventory was available to cache or independently audit for completeness.
